title: [Documentation]: Tutorial page shows asyncio error messages in 'Running the Evaluation' section

comments.body: Fixed this by installing jupyter-asyncio-compat which handles the context variable isolation automatically. Just run `pip install jupyter-asyncio-compat` and it patches the notebook's event loop to prevent these callback errors from appearing in the generated docs.

body: ### Question Type Documentation improvement ### Your Question Documentation Improvement Suggestion in that section below. ### Context and Use Case Documentation Improvement Suggestion in that section below. ### Documentation Consulted _No response_ ### What Have You Tried? ```shell ``` ### Documentation Improvement Suggestion The tutorial page at https://control-arena.aisi.org.uk/tutorial.html appears to have captured asyncio error output in the "Running the Evaluation" section. The rendered HTML shows dozens of error messages that seem to have been inadvertently included when generating the documentation from a Jupyter notebook. They're of the form: ```[01/20/26 13:19:27] ERROR Exception in callback Task.__step() [base_events.py](file:///usr/lib/python3.12/asyncio/base_events.py):[1821](file:///usr/lib/python3.12/asyncio/base_events.py#1821) handle: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/lib/python3.12/asyncio/events.py", line 88, in _run self._context.run(self._callback, *self._args) RuntimeError: cannot enter context: <_contextvars.Context object at 0x75208a7b6500> is already entered ``` According to Claude: `This appears to be a documentation generation issue rather than a code bug - running Inspect AI's eval() function inside a Jupyter notebook's event loop causes these context variable conflicts. ` The tutorial content itself is correct, but the error spam makes it look like the code is broken. Suggested fix: Suppress these error messages when generating the docs Pasted image of error message shown below: ### Additional Context _No response_
